evals.prompt: What are the trending topics in the tech sector right now? Which themes are rising?
evals.expected_output: A plan that: (1) calls getTrends with a tech sector filter, sortBy: 'score', limit: 20, (2) presents a table with Topic | Score | Hits | % Change | Direction, (3) labels each RISING or DECLINING based on % change sign, (4) explicitly calls out the top 3 fastest-rising themes with a brief explanation of what's driving them, (5) includes notable declining themes.

assertions.name: get_trends_called
assertions.description: getTrends is called — not searchSectors or getAnalytics

assertions.name: sorted_by_score_limit_20
assertions.description: Results sorted by score descending with limit 20

assertions.name: table_with_direction_labels
assertions.description: Response includes a table with Score, Hits, % Change, and RISING/DECLINING labels

assertions.name: fastest_rising_called_out
assertions.description: Top 3 fastest-rising themes are explicitly highlighted with explanation

evals.prompt: Show me a mind map of AI in the financial sector
evals.expected_output: A plan that: (1) calls getMindMap with topic: 'AI' or 'artificial intelligence' and a financial sector filter, (2) renders the output as a nested markdown list grouping related sub-themes under parent nodes.

assertions.name: get_mind_map_called
assertions.description: getMindMap is called with the AI topic

assertions.name: nested_list_output
assertions.description: Output is rendered as a nested markdown list with parent and child concept nodes

evals.prompt: How often are companies mentioning tariffs? Show me a term frequency heatmap.
evals.expected_output: A plan that: (1) calls getTermHeatmap with term: 'tariff' (and synonyms if supported), (2) presents results as a table: Term | Company/Quarter | Frequency sorted by frequency descending, (3) flags any companies where the term frequency is spiking compared to prior periods.

assertions.name: get_term_heatmap_called
assertions.description: getTermHeatmap is called with the tariff term

assertions.name: table_sorted_by_frequency
assertions.description: Results presented as a table sorted by frequency descending

assertions.name: spikes_flagged
assertions.description: Companies with notably spiking term frequency are called out
